title: Synergistic effects of antimicrobial peptide KRR-N1-5W6L and thymol on the inactivation of methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus in chilled beef.
author_string: Ma, Maomao, Song, Jiayi, Wu, Yijian, Hu, Yan, Xiao, Yunzhu et al.
abstract: The synergistic effect of antibacterial compounds offers a promising approach for controlling microbial contamination in food. The present study explored the synergistic antibacterial effects of thymol (THY), eugenol, citral and cinnamaldehyde in combination with the antimicrobial peptide KRR-N1-5W6L (KN5) against methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA). Among the combinations, only KN5 and THY exhibited a strong synergistic effect against MRSA as evidenced by a fractional inhibitory concentration index (FICI) of 0.375. Compared to KN5 or THY alone, their combination exhibited markedly enhanced bactericidal performance. Antibacterial mechanism analyses revealed that KN5 and THY synergistically compromised cell structure integrity, causing serious leakage of alkaline phosphatase, nucleic acid and protein, along with the depletion of the proton motive force. Furthermore, THY enhanced the KN5-induced ROS generation and DNA binding affinity, leading to cell death. Additionally, KN5 and THY synergistically suppressed microbial proliferation and delayed the changes in pH, TBARS, and color deterioration in chilled beef without affecting sensory properties. Our results suggested that this combination could be an effective strategy for beef preservation.
